turns.speaker: user
turns.text: improve this text: This study offers a twofold contribution. First, while physical IT infrastructure has long been viewed as a dimensions of IT capability in most studies, this study asserts that the advent of digital technologies, such as cloud computing, has altered this traditional perspective, particularly in the context of hospitals during the process of digital transformation. In this study, physical IT infrastructure is considered an “IT resource”, while IT architecture, IT human capability, and IT relationship capability are regarded as the dimensions of hospital-specific IT capabilities. Second, while physical IT infrastructure in a hospital supports the dimensions of IT capabilities as a platform and shared resource, it does not have a direct influence on digital transformation. Instead, it influences digital transformation indirectly through IT capability dimensions and strategic alignment.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: This research makes a dual contribution. Firstly, previous studies have focused on physical IT infrastructure as a critical component of IT capability; however, this investigation contends that the advent of digital technologies, specifically cloud computing, has changed this perspective, particularly in the context of hospitals undergoing digital transformation. Within this framework, physical IT infrastructure is repositioned as an “IT resource,” with hospital-specific IT capabilities centered on IT architecture, IT human capability, and IT relationship capability. Secondly, physical IT infrastructure acts as a foundational and shared resource for the IT capabilities, but its influence on digital transformation is indirect and routed through strategic alignment and IT capability dimensions.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: Second, while physical IT infrastructure in a hospital supports the dimensions of IT capabilities as a platform and shared resource, it does not have a direct influence on digital transformation. Instead, it influences digital transformation indirectly through IT capability dimensions and strategic alignment.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: Secondly, although physical IT infrastructure in a hospital serves as a platform and shared resource for IT capabilities, it does not directly impact digital transformation. Instead, it has an indirect effect on digital transformation through strategic alignment and IT capability dimensions.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: However, despite the growing interest and adoption of digital solutions in Iranian healthcare, hospitals still face various challenges in maintaining and equipping physical IT infrastructure, which is essential for the efficient integration and support of digital transformation.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: Despite the increasing adoption and interest in digital solutions in Iranian healthcare, hospitals encounter several obstacles in maintaining and equipping their physical IT infrastructure, which is vital for the smooth integration and support of digital transformation.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: This study focuses on hospitals in Iran due to the increasing attention and interest in digital transformation in Iranian healthcare, particularly in response to the Covid-19 pandemic. The pandemic has highlighted the need for efficient and effective digital tools in healthcare, leading to a surge in the adoption of telemedicine, electronic prescriptions, and other digital health solutions. For example, the Social Insurance Organization, which is one of the largest health insurance companies in Iran, announced that from April to the end of October 2022, 97% of prescriptions of insured patients of this company were issued electronically (Rahimimoghadam, 2022). However, despite the growing interest and adoption of digital solutions in Iranian healthcare, hospitals still face various challenges in maintaining and equipping physical IT infrastructure, which is essential for the efficient integration and support of digital transformation. These challenges include high costs of acquiring and maintaining complex IT systems and inadequate infrastructure. To overcome these challenges, it is essential to understand the role and mechanism of physical IT infrastructure in supporting digital transformation and its integration with other IT capabilities. Addressing this knowledge gap will be crucial for effective management of costly IT infrastructure and successful digital transformation in Iranian hospitals.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: The focus of this study is on Iranian hospitals due to their increasing attention and interest in digital transformation, particularly in response to the Covid-19 pandemic. The pandemic has proven the urgent need for efficient digital tools in healthcare, leading to the widespread adoption of telemedicine, electronic prescriptions, and other digital health solutions. For instance, the Social Insurance Organization, one of Iran's largest health insurance companies, reported that from April to October 2022, 97% of prescriptions were electronically issued for insured patients (Rahimimoghadam, 2022). Despite this increasing interest and adoption of digital solutions in Iranian healthcare, hospitals face several challenges in maintaining and equipping their physical IT infrastructure, which is essential for supporting and integrating digital transformation. Some of these challenges include the high costs of purchasing and maintaining complex IT systems, alongside inadequate infrastructure. Addressing this knowledge gap and comprehending the role and mechanism of physical IT infrastructure in supporting digital transformation and its integration with other IT capabilities will be essential for effectively managing the costly IT infrastructure and achieving successful digital transformation in Iranian hospitals.
